R Notebook

library(brms)
library(tidyverse)
library(BayesFactor)
library(BayesianId)
data("penguins")
test_data = na.omit(penguins) %>% rename(y = body_mass, x1 = flipper_len, x2 = bill_len, f1 = island, f2 = sex)
test_data = test_data |> dplyr::mutate(y_bin = test_data$f2, y_cens = ifelse(y > 5000, 5000, y), censoring = (y > 5000))

levels(test_data$f1) |> print()
[1] "Biscoe"    "Dream"     "Torgersen"
levels(test_data$f2) |> print()
[1] "female" "male"  
nrow(test_data) |> print()
[1] 333
tic = Sys.time()
bar = breg(y ~ x1 + x2, 
           data = test_data,
           family = "normal_linear",
           #family = "right_censored_linear",
           #family = "bernoulli_logistic",
           chains = 4,
           iter = 10000,
           center = TRUE
           )
toc = Sys.time()
print(toc - tic)
Time difference of 28.27409 secs
barfight = glm(y ~ x1 + x2, data = BayesianId:::prepare_data(BayesianId:::parse_formula(y ~ x1 + x2), test_data), family = gaussian())
barfight |> coef() |> round(3)
(Intercept)          x1          x2 
   4207.057      48.890       4.959 
Barsoom = lapreg(y_cens ~ x1 + x2, data = test_data, family = "right_censored_linear")
(Intercept)          x1          x2       sigma 
4196.696048   48.430918    2.723129  381.520725 
                b0          b.1          b.2        sigma
b0    -0.002060044  0.005035716  0.001315591    0.1219959
b.1    0.005035716 -0.329308459 -0.082792052    2.2709698
b.2    0.001315591 -0.082792052 -0.058946104    0.5817044
sigma  0.121995851  2.270969813  0.581704392 -553.5935187
Barsoom$optim_fit$par |> round(3)
(Intercept)          x1          x2       sigma 
   4196.696      48.431       2.723     381.521 
print(Barsoom)
lapreg model object
Call:
lapreg(formula = y ~ x1 + x2, data = test_data, family = "normal_linear")
summary(Barsoom)
Call:
lapreg(formula = y ~ x1 + x2, data = test_data, family = "normal_linear")

Estimates:
coef(Barsoom) |> round(3)
Intercept        x1        x2     sigma 
 4207.064    48.778     5.115   390.178 
coef(barfight) |> round(3)
(Intercept)          x1          x2 
   4207.057      48.890       4.959 
vcov(Barsoom) |> round(5)
          Intercept       x1       x2
Intercept   457.173  0.00000  0.00000
x1            0.000  4.05801 -6.75237
x2            0.000 -6.75237 26.47814
vcov(barfight) |> round(5)
            (Intercept)       x1       x2
(Intercept)    464.7553  0.00000  0.00000
x1               0.0000  4.13799 -6.92630
x2               0.0000 -6.92630 27.18064
vcov(bar) |> round(5)
          Intercept       x1       x2
Intercept 461.59805  0.10199 -0.56875
x1          0.10199  4.07914 -6.81729
x2         -0.56875 -6.81729 26.87604
posterior_interval(Barsoom) |> round(3)
confint(barfight) |> round(3)
Waiting for profiling to be done...
               2.5 %   97.5 %
(Intercept) 4164.804 4249.310
x1            44.903   52.877
x2            -5.260   15.177
confint(barfight) |> class()
Waiting for profiling to be done...
[1] "matrix" "array" 
terms(Barsoom)
y ~ x1 + x2
attr(,"variables")
list(y, x1, x2)

x1  1  0
x2  0  1

[1] 1

list(y, x1, x2)

names(Barsoom)
 [1] "optim_fit"      "mu"             "sigma"         
 [4] "Sigma"          "model_name"     "intercept_only"
 [7] "formula"        "formula_info"   "stan_data"     
[10] "data"           "center"         "coef_names"    
[13] "call"          
model.frame(Barsoom) |> head()
plot(Barsoom)

plot(bar)

library(BayesFactor)
library(dplyr)
n = 8
mu_a = 10
mu_b = 13
sigma = 1

df = data.frame(group = c("a", "b") |> rep(each = n),
                y = c(rnorm(n = n, mean = mu_a, sd = sigma),
                      rnorm(n = n, mean = mu_b, sd = sigma))
                )

t.test(y ~ group, data = df, var.equal = TRUE)

    Two Sample t-test

data:  y by group
t = -5.0244, df = 14, p-value = 0.0001859
alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group a and group b is not equal to 0
95 percent confidence interval:
 -4.612189 -1.852567
sample estimates:
mean in group a mean in group b 
       9.500422       12.732800 
ttestBF(formula = y ~ group, data = df)
Bayes factor analysis
--------------
[1] Alt., r=0.707 : 113.2834 ±0%

Against denominator:
  Null, mu1-mu2 = 0 
---
Bayes factor type: BFindepSample, JZS
1/ttestBF(formula = y ~ group, data = df)
Bayes factor analysis
--------------
[1] Null, mu1-mu2=0 : 0.008827418 ±0%

Against denominator:
  Alternative, r = 0.707106781186548, mu =/= 0 
---
Bayes factor type: BFindepSample, JZS
